name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (Flows X-Force Categorization)
notes:
This Building Block defines communication with a potential hostile host, categorized by F-Force.

Note: X-Force categorization is available for free of use. The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (Flows X-Force Categorization) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local
when the flow matches HTTP Host is not N/A

when the flow matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY("HTTP Host") in ('Anonymization Services','Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Spam URLs', 'Cryptocurrency Mining', 'Bots', 'Phishing URLs') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (Flows X-Force Categorization) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Local to Local
AND when the flow matches HTTP Host is not N/A

AND when the flow matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY("HTTP Host") in ('Anonymization Services','Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Spam URLs', 'Cryptocurrency Mining', 'Bots', 'Phishing URLs') AQL filter query
